keyFindings: SQLite/FTS5 is already native; only vector JSON parse/cosine/sort scales across the eligible set in JavaScript.
At 1,290 bundles, tokenizer and weighted RRF are bounded and do not justify a Rust boundary.
ANN is a credible growth feature but needs filter-aware design, exact rescoring, recall gates, and benchmark evidence.
Rust is not necessary for ANN because mature native engines already expose JavaScript, WASM, SQLite, or service surfaces.
A pinned SQLite vector extension is worth a spike because it removes vector JSON transfer and composes with the existing database.
Authoring that extension in Rust is not justified; extension loading, packaging, filtering, and maturity are the real risks.
Local inference adds offline, privacy, cost, and reproducibility value and clears the present-day materiality gate.
Use Node ONNX Runtime for the MVP; Rust becomes attractive only as a shared multimodal inference core.
Continuous dirty-set indexing is valuable, but a TypeScript Chokidar shell plus reconciliation is sufficient today.
Batch-aware embedding scheduling is material and may later live beside a justified native inference core.
Palette, perceptual hash, and simple screenshot geometry add validation and duplicate detection, but Sharp makes Rust unnecessary.
CLIP-class screenshot embeddings add new text-to-image and image-to-image retrieval; Rust is optional for the MVP.
Only profile identity, inference, vector encoding/index interfaces, cache keys, and tie-break primitives are coherent cross-system Rust candidates.
Extract a shared Rust core only after a second system commits to the same material native workload and pinned profile.
Binary or in-database vector representation can remove JSON transfer/parsing, but Rust is not necessary.
Existing hashing and deterministic ranking stay TypeScript; a shared artifact cache becomes useful only with local models or multiple consumers.
Use one pure inference/vector core with a thin napi-rs adapter; TypeScript retains behavior and persistence authority.
The Node ONNX/Sharp fallback should ship first and become the pinned byte oracle for a later Rust path.
Strict whole-result parity may prohibit ANN unless fallback uses the same engine/index or policy defines a separate feature contract.
Ship and measure the Node ONNX/Sharp/Chokidar capability baseline before extracting Rust.
Local multimodal inference is the first plausible Rust extraction; existing vector extensions and ANN follow only after evidence gates.
ruledOutDirections: Like-for-like Rust port of native SQLite/FTS5 or the current small JavaScript kernels
